Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Log in as Amy Chen using mortgage_los_login (email: "amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com") to establish the persona context and enable LOS queries. Then retrieve Amy's pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_4d01ebef3a15" or "amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com", limit: 50) to discover her 8 active 2026 loans (plus 7 older loans from 2024-2025 with locks expired 6-18+ months that may need withdrawal): LN-2026-00552 (Teresa Johnson, clear_to_close), LN-2026-00550 (Nancy Gonzalez, underwriting), LN-2026-00607 (Lauren Bennett, processing), LN-2026-00615 (Marisol Reyes, processing), LN-2026-00534 (Kenneth Russell, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00532 (Takeshi Clark, underwriting), LN-2026-00529 (Danielle Lombardi, underwriting), LN-2026-00526 (DeShawn Davis, conditional_approval). All rate locks are expired. Four files have terminated processors (Rachel Kim on Russell and Reyes, Andre Washington on Lombardi and Davis).
OE 2: Search sent emails using search_emails (query: "Larry Harrison" or "Harrison income" or "options" or similar, folder_name: "SENT") to find Amy's email to larry.harrison@gmail.com dated April 7 with subject "Options for LN-2026-00628 based on verified income" containing restructuring options based on the 4506-T transcript showing $72,000 vs $95,000 on tax returns. This email contains the loan number LN-2026-00628 which is critical for the next LOS lookup.
OE 3: Search inbox emails using search_emails (query: "Larry Harrison" or "Harrison" or "income verification" or similar) to discover Larry Harrison's angry reply email (email_id: email_email_f3f274b96451) sent April 7 to elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com with CC to amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com. Larry refuses to accept the $72k transcript figure, insists his $95k returns are correct, demands escalation, and says "Please do not make any changes to my file based on incorrect information." Also discover Elena's earlier emails: her internal flag to Amy ("Larry Harrison income discrepancy") and her outbound email to Larry ("Update on income verification for your loan", CC Amy).
OE 4: Search LOS for the Harrison loan using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00628") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00628") to discover: status is underwriting, loan amount $435,000, conventional type, rate 6.495%, rate lock expiration 2026-04-04 (expired 24 days as of April 28), lender is Pennymac Wholesale, loan ID is los_loan_3a7f88229103. Check outstanding documents using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_3a7f88229103", status: "required") to find 4 required docs: URLA, current W-2, prior W-2, HOI binder.
OE 5: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "48k" or "180k" or "self-employed" or "self-emp" or "LN-2026-00625" or similar) to discover the self-employed borrower file. Key messages: (1) Elena to Amy: "2025 returns show ~180k gross rev but qualifying income is only landing around 48k after write-offs" on LN-2026-00625, (2) Amy asking Camille for a Non-QM bank statement fallback gut check on LN-2026-00625, (3) Camille's response: "bank stmt could work as backup, but it's gonna price worse than agency. I'd expect higher rate + diff reserve/prepay rules depending on outlet", (4) Amy noting borrower is upset about the 48k vs 180k gap. These messages establish the loan number LN-2026-00625 and Camille's Non-QM assessment as the processing path found so far.
OE 6: Search LOS for the self-employed borrower loan using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00625") to discover: borrower is Adrienne Cole (adrienne.cole@gmail.com), status is underwriting, loan amount $393,000, conventional type at UWM (United Wholesale), rate 6.461%, rate lock expiration 2026-04-11 (expired 17 days as of April 28), property at 7629 Tyvola Rd, Charlotte NC. Check outstanding documents using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_7163cb114daf", status: "required") to find 3 required docs: prior W-2, bank statement 1, title commitment. The employer field is null confirming self-employment.
OE 7: Check rate lock status on both key loans using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_3a7f88229103") for Harrison and mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_7163cb114daf") for Cole. Discover both locks are expired: Harrison lock expired April 4 (24 days ago), Cole lock expired April 11 (17 days ago). Both files need relocks once their respective issues are resolved.
OE 8: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Harrison" or "72k" or "95k" or "4506-T" or "transcript" or similar) to find additional context: Elena asking if anyone has seen a transcript come back "this far off" with a "23k gap", Elena confirming she emailed Larry about the lender needing to use 4506-T income, and Amy noting she sent Larry restructure options off 72k verified income on LN-2026-00628.
OE 9: Search emails for borrower complaints or issues on other active files using search_emails (query: "status update" or "concerned" or "complaint" or "Amy" or similar). Discover: (1) Carlos Mendez (carlos.mendez@gmail.com) emailed Amy and Grace on March 8 with subject "Need a real status update on my loan" complaining about confused communication and typos, referencing LN-2026-00609, (2) Tiffany Brooks (tiffbrooks22@yahoo.com) emailed info@keystonemortgage.com (not Amy directly) on March 8 with subject "Concerned about communication on my file" saying Amy has been hard to reach and messages are full of mistakes on LN-2026-00211. Note: LN-2026-00609 is assigned to Derek Moss in the LOS with borrower Terrence Whitfield, but the email narrative references Amy as the LO the borrower interacted with. This is a known universe mismatch between the scenario email layer and base LOS data.
OE 10: Search LOS for outstanding documents across Amy's other active files. For LN-2026-00609 (loan_id: los_loan_6a8fa93e7715), use mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_6a8fa93e7715", status: "required") to discover 6 outstanding items: credit report, paystub 1, prior tax return, drivers license, HOI binder, title commitment. Check additional pipeline loans for missing docs and expired locks using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id or mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status on the other active loan IDs from the pipeline.
OE 11: Search CRM for engagement history on the Harrison file using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Larry Harrison") to find CRM contact crm_contact_2e65491ee430 (larry.harrison@hotmail.com). Then use crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: ["crm_contact_2e65491ee430"]) to find the four tracked engagement notes on Harrison's record: (1) "Income discrepancy noted" (NOTE: 4506-T back at 72k vs 95k on uploaded returns), (2) "Asked ops about transcript gap" (NOTE: asked processing team about mismatch), (3) "Angry reply on income issue" (EMAIL: borrower upset, says returns are correct, pushes back on lower loan amount), (4) "Restructure options sent" (EMAIL: sent lower-loan-amount options based on 72k verified income). Note: Adrienne Cole (the self-employed borrower) does not have a CRM contact record. The self-employed file data is tracked via Slack messages (OE 5) and LOS records (OE 6), not CRM engagements.
OE 12: Update loan notes on the Harrison file using mortgage_los_update_loan (loan_id: "los_loan_3a7f88229103", notes: status update noting the 4506-T transcript discrepancy at $72k vs $95k returns, Larry's angry response demanding escalation, lock expired April 4, and need for Amy to respond and determine next steps) or mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_3a7f88229103", action: "note" or similar, detail: status update content).
OE 13: Update loan notes on the Cole self-employed file using mortgage_los_update_loan (loan_id: "los_loan_7163cb114daf", notes: status update noting self-employed income at ~48k qualifying vs ~180k gross, Camille's Non-QM bank statement fallback assessment, borrower upset about income calculation, lock expired April 11, 3 outstanding docs, and need for Amy to decide conventional vs Non-QM path and respond to borrower) or mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_7163cb114daf", action: "note" or similar, detail: status update content).
OE 14: Update loan notes on other active pipeline files that have significant issues using mortgage_los_update_loan or mortgage_los_add_activity on loans including: LN-2026-00552 (Teresa Johnson, CTC but closing date 4/8 passed, lock expired 4/15), LN-2026-00550 (Nancy Gonzalez, UW at AmeriHome, closing 4/14 passed, lock expired 4/17), LN-2026-00607 (Lauren Bennett, processing, missing paystub and W-2, lock expired 4/18), LN-2026-00615 (Marisol Reyes, processing at UWM, missing bank statement, HOI binder, appraisal, lock expired 3/31, processor Rachel Kim terminated). Each note should reflect current status, expired lock date, missing docs, and processor assignment issues.
OE 15: Compile and report to Amy who still needs a response: (1) Larry Harrison -- unread angry email demanding escalation on 4506-T discrepancy, most urgent, (2) Adrienne Cole -- waiting for explanation of 48k vs 180k qualifying income and decision on Non-QM pivot, (3) Carlos Mendez -- complaint email about slow follow-up on LN-2026-00609, (4) Teresa Johnson -- CTC file 20 days past closing date needs relock and new closing coordination, (5) borrowers on files with terminated processors (Marisol Reyes, Kenneth Russell, Danielle Lombardi, DeShawn Davis) need processor reassignment before any progress can be made. This is the agent's final response to the user and maps to Outcome 2.1 rubric criteria rather than a specific tool call.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

 ✗—✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never found LN-2026-00628 (loan_id: los_loan_3a7f88229103). It searched for 'Harrison' and 'Larry Harrison' in the LOS, which only returned the closed FHA loan LN-2025-00003 (los_loan_5b20ed655bdb). The tool results confirm lock data was retrieved directly from the LOS.
✓Run #5The agent retrieved rate lock data via mortgage_los_get_pipeline calls (which return rate_lock_expiration) and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id calls that include rate lock details. The criteria explicitly allows 'mortgage_los_get_loan calls that return lock data.'
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status eight times across all pipeline loans, including for both the self-employed borrower (LN-2026-00625) and other active files.
23 The model must use a Mortgage LOS document tool (e.g., mortgage_los_get_documents or mortgage_los_get_outstanding_documents) to check for outstanding documents on active files.
tool selection
 ✗—✓✗✗✗ 20%
✗Run #1The agent never called mortgage_los_get_documents or mortgage_los_get_outstanding_documents as dedicated document tools. Document data was incidentally returned within mortgage_los_get_loan calls, but no dedicated document tool was invoked.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_get_documents with loan_id los_loan_7163cb114daf (LN-2026-00625) to check outstanding documents.
✗Run #4The agent never called mortgage_los_get_documents or mortgage_los_get_outstanding_documents. Document information was incidentally available in the mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id responses, but no dedicated document-check tool was invoked. The outstanding document check was done indirectly rather than through the specified tools.
✗Run #5The agent never called mortgage_los_get_documents or mortgage_los_get_outstanding_documents. Document information appeared embedded in the get_loan_by_id responses, but the dedicated document tools were not invoked.
✗Run #6No explicit call to mortgage_los_get_documents or mortgage_los_get_outstanding_documents appears in the trajectory. Document information was obtained as a side effect of mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id returning embedded document arrays, not via a dedicated document tool call.
24 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements, or crm_get_contact) to look up engagement history or contact records.
tool selection
 ✗—✗✗✗✓ 20%
✗Run #1No CRM tool calls (crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements, crm_get_contact, or similar) appear anywhere in the agent trajectory. The agent relied solely on email and Slack searches for engagement history.
—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3No CRM tool calls (crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements, crm_get_contact, or similar) appear anywhere in the trajectory.
✗Run #4The agent called crm_search_leads (not crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements, or crm_get_contact). The crm_search_leads tool is a sales pipeline tool, not an engagement history tool. The agent never queried CRM engagement history for Harrison or the self-employed borrower, missing the interaction timelines captured there.
✗Run #5The trajectory contains no calls to crm_search_contacts, crm_list_engagements, or crm_get_contact. The agent never queried the CRM for engagement history or contact records.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_contacts with full_name 'Larry Harrison' and received a result with his CRM contact record. This satisfies the requirement to use a CRM tool.
25 The model must pass 'LN-2026-00628' as the loan_number parameter (or a query containing 'LN-2026-00628') when calling mortgage_los_get_loan or mortgage_los_search_loans to retrieve the Harrison loan record.
query construction
 ✗—✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never queried 'LN-2026-00628' in the LOS. It searched for 'Harrison' and 'Larry Harrison' which only returned LN-2025-00003 (the old closed loan). No call with loan_number 'LN-2026-00628' was made, so the correct Harrison file was never found.
—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3The agent searched mortgage_los_search_loans with 'Harrison' and 'LN-2026-00625' but never searched for 'LN-2026-00628'. The Harrison loan was never looked up by its loan number in the LOS.
✗Run #4The agent never searched the LOS using 'LN-2026-00628'. It searched by borrower name 'Larry Harrison' and by LO 'Amy Chen' but never found the loan number from the sent email. It searched sent emails for 'Larry Harrison income options' which returned no results — the agent never extracted LN-2026-00628 from the email subject line and never queried the LOS with that loan number.
✗Run #5The agent never searched for 'LN-2026-00628' in mortgage_los_get_loan or mortgage_los_search_loans. It searched for 'Larry Harrison' and 'larry.harrison' but never extracted the loan number from email context to query the LOS directly.
✗Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans with query 'Larry Harrison' (found the old loan LN-2025-00003) but never searched with 'LN-2026-00628'. The agent never discovered the correct loan number for the Harrison file being discussed, so no LOS query with LN-2026-00628 was made.
